question: Define Design Guidelines and explain their importance in UI/UX design.
expectedPoints: Design guidelines are comprehensive documents providing frameworks for consistent, accessible, user-friendly products
They establish patterns, principles, and standards
Importance: ensure consistency, improve usability, accelerate design process, maintain accessibility

question: Explain the four principles of WCAG (POUR) with one example for each.
expectedPoints: Perceivable: Content presented in ways users can perceive (alt text for images)
Operable: Interface must be operable (keyboard navigation)
Understandable: Information must be understandable (consistent language)
Robust: Content works with current and future technologies (valid HTML)

question: What is Material Design? List three key principles of Material Design.
expectedPoints: Material Design is Google's design language
Three principles: Material as metaphor (physical properties), Bold graphic design (typography, grids), Meaningful motion (responsive animations)
Uses grid-based layouts, depth effects, responsive animations

question: Explain the difference between a Style Guide and a Design System with examples.
expectedPoints: Style Guide: Focuses on visual elements only - colors, typography, logos, spacing
Design System: Comprehensive - includes style guide + reusable components + patterns + documentation + governance
Example: Style guide says 'blue #007BFF', Design System includes button components built with that blue

question: What is the 60-30-10 rule in color usage? Explain with an example.
expectedPoints: 60% dominant color (backgrounds/neutral areas)
30% secondary color (furniture, cards, sidebars)
10% accent color (CTAs, highlights, important elements)
Example: 60% white background, 30% gray panels, 10% orange buttons

question: List and briefly explain any five of Nielsen's Usability Heuristics.
expectedPoints: Visibility of system status - keep users informed
Match between system and real world - use familiar language
User control and freedom - easy exit from actions
Consistency and standards - platform conventions
Error prevention - smart defaults
Recognition rather than recall - visible options
Flexibility and efficiency - shortcuts for experts

question: You are designing a banking mobile app. Explain how you would apply design guidelines to ensure accessibility and usability. Discuss specific considerations for color contrast, touch targets, and navigation patterns.
expectedPoints: Color contrast: Ensure 4.5:1 ratio for text, use high contrast for critical actions, test with color-blind users
Touch targets: Minimum 44x44px for all interactive elements, adequate spacing between buttons
Navigation: Consistent placement, clear labels, breadcrumb trails
Accessibility: Screen reader support, keyboard navigation, alternative text
Platform guidelines: Follow Material Design (Android) or HIG (iOS)
Consistency: Same patterns across screens, familiar icons
Error handling: Clear error messages, undo options, confirmation for critical actions

question: Explain what a Design System is and describe its components. How would you implement a Design System for a large organization with multiple product teams?
expectedPoints: Design System definition: Collection of reusable components guided by clear standards
Components: Design tokens (colors, spacing), Style guide, Component library, Pattern library, Documentation, Governance
Implementation: 1) Audit existing design assets 2) Define design tokens 3) Create core components 4) Document usage guidelines 5) Establish governance 6) Train teams 7) Maintain and evolve
Benefits: Consistency across products, faster development, easier collaboration, unified brand experience

question: Discuss the WCAG 2.1 accessibility guidelines. How would you ensure a website meets AA compliance? Provide specific techniques for perceivable, operable, and understandable requirements.
expectedPoints: WCAG 2.1 has three levels: A, AA, AAA
AA compliance requires: 4.5:1 contrast ratio, keyboard accessibility, clear navigation, error identification
Perceivable techniques: Alt text, captions, sufficient contrast, resizable text
Operable techniques: Keyboard navigation, skip links, sufficient time for interactions, seizure prevention
Understandable techniques: Readable content, predictable navigation, error prevention and assistance
Techniques: Use semantic HTML, ARIA labels, focus indicators, form validation messages, consistent layout
